mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.0% show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in residential construction permits (RSAFS) above its 12-month moving average indicates strong housing demand, which correlates with positive equity returns through improved consumer confidence and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in RSAFS above-MA periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 6-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing activity, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO below-MA periods show positive or flat performance (mean > -0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% and decline in FEDFUNDS below 1.5% creates a 'stagflation' risk scenario that predicts negative S&P 500 returns through heightened market volatility and earnings pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in stagflation scenarios show positive or flat performance (mean > -0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates a paradoxical 'soft landing' scenario where economic slowdown is offset by real estate demand, predicting positive S&P 500 returns through sector rotation.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in soft landing scenarios show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200k combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market that drives consumer spending and equity valuations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in strong labor market scenarios show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 combined with declining capacity utilization predicts positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings and sector-specific momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO above-105 periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p-value > 0.1 in t-test against baseline.
